name: The new covenant
notes: Jeremiah prophesies a new covenant the LORD will make with the house of Israel and the house of Judah, unlike the covenant their fathers brake at the Exodus: God will write his law in their hearts, all shall know him, and he will remember their sin no more. Hebrews 8 quotes the oracle in full to show that the better covenant Christ mediates was promised of old, and Hebrews 10 cites it again in part, drawing out the heart-written law and the pardon of sins to seal its argument that one offering perfects the sanctified. Where Hebrews 8 splits Jeremiah's closing verse into a clause on universal knowledge of the Lord (8:11) and a clause on forgiveness (8:12), the single verse Jer 31:34 links to both. Hebrews 10:16 condenses the heart-and-mind clause of Jer 31:33, and Hebrews 10:17 repeats the remembrance-of-sins-no-more of Jer 31:34. The apostle follows the Septuagint reading at points ("I regarded them not," "merciful to their unrighteousness"), a complementary vantage on the same prophecy left as a matter for further study.
